message:
feat(fnp-python): native tile/ravel/clip fast paths (br-franken_numpy-hswa)

Ports three hswa passthroughs to native UFuncArray kernels with numpy
fallbacks preserving the full dispatch surface.

tile: real numeric inputs with non-negative int / int-list reps route
through UFuncArray::tile. Scalar reps (single int), complex dtype,
negative reps, and non-integer reps fall back to np.tile.

ravel: default C-order flattening of real/complex numeric arrays uses
UFuncArray::ravel. F/A/K order or non-numeric (object/string/structured)
inputs fall back to np.ravel.

clip: scalar f64 min/max bounds on real numeric inputs route through
UFuncArray::clip. None bounds (one-sided clip), broadcast min/max
arrays, complex dtype, explicit  buffers, and any extra kwargs
(casting, where, dtype, …) fall back to np.clip.

Existing tile_* / ravel_* / clip_* parity tests (13 total) all pass
unchanged — verified via 'cargo test -p fnp-python --lib tile ravel clip'
and full-suite 395/395 green under parallel cargo test.
